title: Crate scaffold, prolly tree node types, and in-memory node store
purpose: Establish the haematite crate with the foundational prolly tree data structures. This brief delivers the node types (leaf and internal), deterministic serialisation, BLAKE3 content hashing, rolling hash boundary detection, the NodeStore trait, and an in-memory store for testing. Everything else in the core cluster builds on these types.
task: Create the haematite crate with Cargo.toml (blake3, zstd, beamr dependencies). Define LeafNode and InternalNode types with deterministic serialisation and BLAKE3 hashing. Implement rolling hash boundary detection with configurable target node size. Define the NodeStore trait and implement MemoryStore as an in-memory HashMap<Hash, Node>. The lib.rs re-exports public API types. This brief builds the representation layer only — tree traversal and mutation operations are CORE-002.

requirements.title: Crate manifest and module structure
requirements.spec: Create the haematite crate with Cargo.toml listing blake3, zstd, and beamr as dependencies. Create lib.rs with public API re-exports. Create tree/mod.rs and store/mod.rs as module roots containing only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
acceptance: crates/haematite/Cargo.toml exists with blake3, zstd, beamr in [dependencies]
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs compiles and re-exports tree and store modules
tree/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic
store/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic
cargo check -p haematite succeeds

requirements.title: LeafNode type with sorted key-value pairs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a LeafNode type that holds a sorted Vec of (key, value) pairs where keys are byte vectors and values are byte vectors. THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce sort order on construction — constructing a LeafNode with unsorted keys is an error. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow duplicate keys within a single LeafNode.
acceptance: LeafNode::new(entries) returns Ok when entries are sorted by key
LeafNode::new(entries) returns Err when entries are unsorted
LeafNode::new(entries) returns Err when entries contain duplicate keys
LeafNode::entries() returns the pairs in sorted key order
LeafNode implements Debug

requirements.title: InternalNode type with sorted key-childHash pairs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an InternalNode type that holds a sorted Vec of (key, child_hash) pairs where keys are byte vectors and child_hash values are 32-byte BLAKE3 hashes. THE SYSTEM SHALL enforce sort order on construction. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow duplicate keys within a single InternalNode.
acceptance: InternalNode::new(children) returns Ok when children are sorted by key
InternalNode::new(children) returns Err when children are unsorted
InternalNode::children() returns entries in sorted key order
Child hash values are [u8; 32]
InternalNode implements Debug

requirements.title: Deterministic node serialisation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL serialise LeafNode and InternalNode to a canonical byte sequence that is deterministic: the same node contents always produce the same bytes. THE SYSTEM SHALL use a simple length-prefixed binary format (not serde/bincode) to guarantee byte-level determinism across Rust compiler versions. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT depend on HashMap iteration order or any non-deterministic source.
acceptance: Serialising the same LeafNode twice produces identical byte sequences
Serialising the same InternalNode twice produces identical byte sequences
Deserialising a serialised node produces an equal node (round-trip)
Serialisation format is length-prefixed, not relying on serde

requirements.title: BLAKE3 content hashing for nodes
requirements.spec: WHEN a node is serialised, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute its BLAKE3 hash from the serialised byte sequence and return it as a 32-byte Hash type. THE SYSTEM SHALL define Hash as a newtype over [u8; 32] with Display (hex), Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, and Ord. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use any input other than the serialised bytes to compute the hash — content is identity (P1).
acceptance: Hash is a newtype over [u8; 32]
Hash implements Display as hex string
Hash implements Eq, Ord, std::hash::Hash for use as HashMap key
LeafNode::hash() returns the BLAKE3 hash of LeafNode::serialise()
InternalNode::hash() returns the BLAKE3 hash of InternalNode::serialise()
Two nodes with identical contents produce identical hashes
Two nodes with different contents produce different hashes

requirements.title: Rolling hash boundary detection
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a rolling hash function over the key stream that determines node boundaries. WHEN the rolling hash of a key meets a configurable threshold, THE SYSTEM SHALL place a boundary (split point) at that key. THE SYSTEM SHALL parameterise the threshold by a target node size (default: 4KB). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use random or time-dependent inputs — boundary detection must be history-independent (CN5, C8).
acceptance: BoundaryDetector::new(target_size) creates a detector with the given target
BoundaryDetector::is_boundary(key) returns true when the rolling hash meets the threshold
The same key sequence always produces the same boundary positions
Changing the target size changes where boundaries fall
A stream of 1000 random keys produces boundaries at roughly target_size intervals

requirements.title: NodeStore trait and MemoryStore implementation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a NodeStore trait with get(hash) -> Option<Node> and put(node) -> Hash methods. THE SYSTEM SHALL implement MemoryStore as a HashMap<Hash, serialised_bytes> that satisfies NodeStore. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require NodeStore implementations to handle compression — that is the DiskStore's concern (CORE-004).
acceptance: NodeStore trait has get(&self, hash: &Hash) -> Option<Node>
NodeStore trait has put(&mut self, node: &Node) -> Hash
MemoryStore::put stores a node and returns its BLAKE3 hash
MemoryStore::get retrieves a node by its hash
MemoryStore::get returns None for unknown hashes
MemoryStore implements Debug

requirements.title: Node enum and public type exports
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a Node enum with Leaf(LeafNode) and Internal(InternalNode) variants. THE SYSTEM SHALL implement hash() and serialise()/deserialise() on the Node enum delegating to the inner type. THE SYSTEM SHALL export Hash, Node, LeafNode, InternalNode, NodeStore, MemoryStore, and BoundaryDetector from the crate root via lib.rs.
acceptance: Node::Leaf(leaf) delegates hash() to LeafNode::hash()
Node::Internal(internal) delegates hash() to InternalNode::hash()
Node round-trips through serialise/deserialise
All public types are accessible from haematite::* in lib.rs

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement tree traversal or mutation — those are CORE-002
SHALL NOT implement disk storage or compression — that is CORE-004
SHALL NOT implement WAL buffer or durable writes — those are CORE-005
SHALL NOT use serde or bincode for node serialisation — determinism requires a controlled binary format
SHALL NOT implement branching, merging, or fork operations
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests
cargo clippy -p haematite produces no errors
grep -r 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/ --include='*.rs' -l finds no non-test files
No file in crates/haematite/src/ exceeds 500 lines
